name: requestSelfUnlink writes support_link_ended notice with graceEndsAt instead of graceDays → self-unlink route 500s
description: The supportee self-unlink path is broken at runtime. apps/api/src/services/supportership-revocation.ts:77-88 (requestSelfUnlink) writes a support_link_ended visibility notice with payload {supporteePersonId, revokedAt, graceEndsAt}. createVisibilityNotice (apps/api/src/services/visibility-moment-projections.ts:15-34) inserts the row then re-parses the payload via visibilityMomentPayloadSchema.safeParse and THROWS 'Visibility notice payload failed validation after insert' when it fails. supportLinkEndedPayloadSchema (packages/schemas/src/visibility-contract.ts:132-138) requires graceDays (non-negative int), NOT graceEndsAt — so the parse fails (graceEndsAt is stripped as an unknown key; required graceDays is missing). Because createVisibilityNotice is called inside the requestSelfUnlink db.transaction, the throw rolls back the entire revoke (supportership.revokedAt + contract status='revoked' are both reverted) and the route handler apps/api/src/routes/visibility.ts:107 returns 500. Proven at runtime: visibilityMomentPayloadSchema.safeParse({supporteePersonId, revokedAt, graceEndsAt}).success === false ('graceDays expected number, received undefined'); the same parse with {…, graceDays: 7} succeeds. The schema comment names inngest/functions/supportership-revocation.ts as the sole intended producer, and that Inngest function writes the schema-correct {…, graceDays: SUPPORTERSHIP_GRACE_DAYS} — so the service producer is the outlier. The bug is latent because the happy path is untested: services/supportership-revocation.integration.test.ts only injects failures via a failing-db Proxy, and the WI-1126 T7a test (services/supportership-revocation.auth.integration.test.ts) only exercises the forbidden supporter path, which throws ForbiddenError at :45-47 before reaching the transaction. Found incidentally while writing the WI-1126 S5 break-tests.
acceptanceCriteria:
Root cause (confirmed): requestSelfUnlink writes a support_link_ended notice with payload {supporteePersonId, revokedAt, graceEndsAt}; createVisibilityNotice re-parses post-insert via visibilityMomentPayloadSchema and THROWS when it fails (supportLinkEndedPayloadSchema requires graceDays:int, not graceEndsAt). The throw is inside the revoke db.transaction -> full rollback -> route visibility.ts:107 returns 500. Proven at runtime: parse of the service payload fails ("graceDays expected number, received undefined"); parse with graceDays:7 passes. Latent because the happy path is untested.

Decision (RULED 2026-06-30 - Option A): the Inngest function (inngest/functions/supportership-revocation.ts) is the SOLE producer of support_link_ended, written after the 7-day grace-window sleep. The supporter is notified only after grace, never immediately.

Fix: DELETE the createVisibilityNotice('support_link_ended', ...) call from requestSelfUnlink entirely - do NOT merely change its payload. requestSelfUnlink keeps the revoke + contract update + supportership_revoked audit event + RevocationNotice return; the route keeps its safeSend(app/supportership.unlinked) dispatch that drives the Inngest producer. Deleting the bad write removes the crash by construction. Drop any now-orphaned graceEndsAt local. NB: revokedAt/access is cut immediately today; grace delays only the notice - if immediate access-cutoff is itself wrong that is a separate item.

Test (red-green, the gap that hid this): add a happy-path integration test in services/supportership-revocation.integration.test.ts (real Neon DB, no internal mocks) calling requestSelfUnlink as the SUPPORTEE on a clean db, asserting (a) it resolves, (b) supportership.revokedAt set + contract status='revoked', (c) NO support_link_ended notice is written synchronously (Inngest owns it). Current code -> fails (throws/rollback); after the deletion -> passes. Verify: pnpm exec nx run api:integration-api green; api:typecheck + api:lint clean.
riskImpact: P1 user-facing breakage: the supportee self-unlink route (POST /visibility/links/:id/revoke) 500s on EVERY call — the schema-validation throw inside the revoke db.transaction rolls back supportership.revokedAt + contract status, so a supportee CANNOT end a supportership at all (autonomy/privacy-relevant). No data corruption (full rollback); blast radius = the self-unlink flow only. Fix is a deletion (Option A, ruled 2026-06-30): remove the schema-invalid synchronous support_link_ended write; the Inngest function remains the sole, schema-correct producer after the grace window. Regression risk low — covered by the new happy-path integration test (red-green).
